evals.prompt: We have an Angular 18 app with some shared components but our designers just hand us Figma links and we rebuild everything from scratch. We want to use AI to speed things up.
evals.expected_output: Full audit report scoring low on Rings 2-3, moderate on Ring 1. Angular-specific guidance. Quick wins lead with CLAUDE.md and Figma MCP.

assertions.text: Report contains Ring Scores table for all 5 rings

evals.prompt: I'm a solo dev with a React/Next.js project using shadcn/ui and Tailwind. How AI-ready is my setup?
evals.expected_output: Audit recognizing shadcn provides high Ring 1 maturity. Ring 2 scored 0 (no Figma). Targets adjusted for solo dev. No Angular playbook.

assertions.text: Report contains Ring Scores table for all 5 rings
Acknowledges shadcn as strong component infrastructure
Mentions shadcn MCP or registry as strength/win
No-Figma treated as gap not blocker

evals.prompt: Our team of 12 has a mature design system with tokens, Storybook, and Figma libraries, but we haven't done anything with AI yet. Where should we start?
evals.expected_output: Rings 1-2 scored high (3-4), Ring 3 scored 0-1. Quick wins focus on Ring 3. Does not recommend redoing tokens/components.

assertions.text: Report contains Ring Scores table for all 5 rings
Quick wins focus on Ring 3 (AI enablement)
Does NOT recommend major token/component work
Mentions Code Connect as key unlock

evals.prompt: We have a detailed application design in Figma. Part of the application is already functionally coded in React, but we need to refactor to Angular and use the design in Figma.
evals.expected_output: Migration scenario identified. Figma as stable bridge. Angular-specific guidance. Action plan starts with Figma, not React code porting.

assertions.text: Report contains Ring Scores table for all 5 rings
Identifies this as a migration scenario
Figma identified as key stable/transferable asset
Ring 1 components scored low (0-2) for Angular target
Action plan starts with design asset stabilization
Does NOT recommend porting React patterns to Angular
Recommends Figma MCP + Code Connect for Angular
